title: Senior Software Engineer
jd_text: Senior Software Engineer - £85k + bonus | FinTech/ Banking | Java, Kotlin, AWS | Leeds/hybridAbout the business:G.Digital have partnered with a FinTech/Bank making moves in the payments industry by creating revolutionary digital first solutions. They are the epitome of modern banking via mobile and online platforms, with a streamlined, app-based experience and no traditional branch network.About the role:🔥Design, build, and support scalable backend services and integrations across the Banking Services platform, owning complex features end-to-end from initial design through production release and continuous enhancement.🔥Deliver high-quality, thoroughly tested, and high-performance code; lead and contribute to code reviews to uphold engineering standards and best practices, while championing the responsible use of AI-assisted development tools to enhance delivery, quality, and developer experience.Required Skills: 🔧Experienced backend engineer specialising in distributed systems, with strong Kotlin/Java skills and a focus on scalable, high-performance design.⚙️Confident working in cloud-native environments (ideally AWS) with hands-on experience in containers and Kubernetes.🧰Champions engineering excellence through code reviews, best practices, and clear, collaborative communication.🔧Bonus: gRPC and AWS service experience (SQS, DynamoDB, Lambda, etc.), exposure to AI-assisted tooling, and background in regulated or financial environments.What's in it for you?📍Great Salary: up to £85k + bonus📍Private healthcare📍6% pension📍Life assurance x 4 salary 📍28 + bank holidays + buy and sell options📍 Hybrid working - Leeds x 2 days a weekIf you'd like to find out more, drop me a message on:c.stoddart@g-digital.co.ukSenior Software Engineer - £85k + bonus | FinTech/ Banking | Java, Kotlin, AWS | Leeds/hybrid

title: Head Of Of Engineering
jd_text: About Torch DentalTorch Dental is a high-growth, venture-backed vertical SaaS company transforming how dental practices run their businesses. Our mission is to give every dental office the modern software, automation, and visibility they need to operate efficiently and profitably.We are launching our second product in 2026 and will be expanding into the broader practice-operations layers such as Finance, HR, payroll, benefits, and back-office workflows—building toward a full dental practice operating system.Torch Dental is at the Series B stage with strong revenue growth, rapidly expanding products, and increasingly exciting engineering challenges. This role is an opportunity to lead the engineering team that architects and maintains the platform that services thousands of dental practices.Role OverviewWe are hiring a Head of Engineering to lead Torch’s engineering organization through its next stage of scale. This is a top technical leadership role at Torch, responsible for the engineering organization, architecture, and technical strategy.ResponsibilitiesEstablish the architectural vision and engineering strategy across products.Drive decisions on platform modularization, infrastructure, data, reliability, and integrations.Lead and grow an engineering organization capable of executing across multiple product lines in parallel.Lead existing product feature development: catalog, search, ordering, payments, vendor integrations, logistics.Oversee new product launches: Architecture and development strategy and execution of launching and scaling new products.Lead engineers in building regulatory compliant and scalable multi product platform. Own engineering excellence: velocity, reliability, architecture, security, and quality.Lead team of NYC based engineers and add team capacity expansion initiatives.Recruit and coach backend, frontend, and other engineering talent.Serve as engineering representative working cross functionally with all other business teams.What We Are Looking For8+ years experience building and scaling engineering teams in high-growth SaaS.3+ years in leadership and people management with a track record of success coaching and development of team members.Demonstrated architectural leadership in complex, integrated environments.Strong cross-functional partnership with Product, GTM, and Customers.Compensation And BenefitsCompensation: $250k to $325k cashEquity awardComprehensive medical, dental, and wellness benefits401k benefitsUnlimited paid time off (PTO)Maternity and Paternity leaveFully stocked pantry and fridge for hybrid employeesAn employee-driven culture with happy hours, team events, and other initiatives to celebrate our many successes$250,000 - $325,000 a yearWe are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Senior Electrical Design Engineer
jd_text: Senior Electrical Design EngineerLocation: Farringdon, London (3 days office / 2 days home) Salary: £60,000 (flexible for the right candidate) Benefits: To be confirmedA well-established building services consultancy in Central London is seeking an experienced Senior Electrical Design Engineer to join their growing team.This is an excellent opportunity to play a key role in delivering high-quality electrical designs across predominantly residential projects, with exposure to commercial schemes.The RoleYou will be heavily involved in RIBA Stages 3-4, producing detailed electrical designs and coordinating with multidisciplinary teams to ensure technical compliance and smooth project delivery.Key ResponsibilitiesDevelop electrical building services designs across RIBA Stages 3-4Produce detailed drawings and technical documentation using AutoCAD and RevitUndertake lighting calculations using Dialux or ReluxPrepare Electrical O&M manuals and technical submissionsEnsure compliance with British Standards and UK Building RegulationsCoordinate with mechanical, structural and architectural teamsAttend client and design meetingsSupport delivery within agreed timelines and budgetsRequirementsDegree (or equivalent) in Electrical or Building Services EngineeringProven experience in electrical building services designStrong residential project experience (commercial exposure desirable)Proficiency in AutoCAD and RevitExperience with Dialux or ReluxStrong knowledge of British Standards and UK regulationsExcellent communication and coordination skillsThis role offers genuine progression and the opportunity to work on high-quality London-based developments.ersg are an equal opportunities employer; we are committed to promoting equality of opportunity for all job applicants. We do not discriminate against applicants on the basis of age, sex, race, disability, pregnancy, marital status, sexual orientation, gender reassignment or religious background; all decisions are based on merit.

title: Mid-level Software Engineer
jd_text: 🚀 We’re hiring a Mid-level Full Stack Engineer at Birl 🚀At Birl, we’re building the future of returns for e-commerce. Our mission is simple: make returns effortless for shoppers, valuable for brands, and better for the planet.We’re a small, ambitious startup tackling a problem that touches millions of online purchases every day. There’s a lot to build, plenty of unknowns, and huge opportunity ahead. If you enjoy ownership, moving fast, and figuring things out as you go, you’ll feel very at home here.Now is a brilliant time to join as we scale our platform, deepen our integrations, and work towards becoming the go-to global returns solution 🌍♻️The roleWe’re looking for a Mid-level/weight Full Stack Software Engineer to join our small engineering team. You’ll work collaboratively in the tech team and the wider team to build and evolve Birl’s core product, from customer-facing returns flows to merchant dashboards and backend services.This is a hands-on role with real ownership. You’ll help shape technical decisions, influence how we build, and ship things that customers actually use.What you’ll be doing:Designing, building and shipping features across the full stack, from user interfaces through to backend services and data layers.Developing and maintaining scalable backend services and APIs in Node.js, with a strong focus on reliability, performance and security.Contributing to high-quality customer and merchant experiences using React and Next.js.Building and maintaining integrations with e-commerce platforms, particularly Shopify.Working with GCP to deploy, monitor and scale systems in a production environment.Taking ownership of features from early discovery and technical design through to launch, monitoring and iteration.Contributing to sensible engineering practices, documentation and code quality as the team and codebase grow.What we’re looking for:Strong commercial experience working full stack with JavaScript, including React or Next.js and Node.js.A genuine interest in backend engineering, with solid experience building APIs, integrations and production systems.Experience working in e-commerce, including platform integrations such as Shopify.Good technical judgement and the ability to make pragmatic decisions within an evolving architecture.Comfortable working in a small, fast-moving team with ambiguity and changing priorities.A proactive, delivery-focused mindset balanced with care for quality, testing and maintainability.Someone who enjoys ownership and takes pride in seeing features through from idea to production.Startup experience is a plus, but not essential if you thrive in fast-paced environments.Why Birl?High impact role with genuine ownership from day one.Small team, no layers, and lots of influence.Meaningful work at the intersection of e-commerce and sustainability.Big ambition, fast learning, and plenty of room to grow with the company.Stages:Drop us an email with your CV at hello@wearebirl.com with the subject: "Birl 🟣" to show you've at least read this part of the job descriptionInitial 15 min chat with a member of our teamA 30-60 min session with one of our engineersMeet one (or both) of our foundersWe aim to get back to you within a few days no matter which stage you're at - to the best of our ability anyway! If you’re excited by early-stage energy, enjoy solving real problems, and want to help shape a product and team from the inside - we’d love to hear from you! Compensation: £50-70k (depending on experience)Location: Hybrid (London-based) Disclaimer: We're currently not working with any recruiters.

title: Talent Acquisition Lead
jd_text:
About The TeamAt Vintage Cash Cow and Arcavindi, our People Team is passionate about creating an exceptional employee experience, from first interaction to long-term growth. Within the team, our Talent Acquisition function is dedicated to finding, engaging, and hiring people who share our purpose: creating a world where everything has value and nothing is wasted.We partner across Operations, Logistics, Tech, and Commercial teams to ensure every hire strengthens our culture and fuels our mission: to build the world’s largest trading platform for pre-loved items, driven by people and data.We don’t just hire for skills. We hire people who add to our culture — bringing curiosity, ownership, and fresh thinking to help us scale sustainably.About the role: As a Talent Acquisition Lead / Manager, you own hiring performance for your market. You are accountable for delivery outcomes, stakeholder confidence, sourcing effectiveness, and process excellence across your region (UK or EU).This is a leadership role with hands-on credibility. While you won’t carry the same volume as a TA Partner, you remain close enough to delivery to coach, unblock, and elevate standards.You’ll lead and develop TA Partners and Resourcing Partners, ensuring structured hiring, strong pipelines, and a consistent, values-led candidate experience.You are the bridge between global TA strategy and market execution. ________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Getting Started... Build a deep understanding of your market’s workforce plan and hiring priorities. Establish trusted relationships with senior stakeholders and hiring managers. Review current pipeline health, time-to-hire, and performance metrics. Align your team on sourcing focus, SLAs, and quality standards.Establishing Your Impact… Take accountability for market-level hiring performance and delivery outcomes. Coach TA Partners and Resourcing Partners to improve consistency and efficiency. Drive clarity in intake sessions, role scoping, and success profiling. Strengthen proactive pipelining across recurring and critical roles. Ensure structured, inclusive hiring practices are embedded across your region.Driving Excellence… Own market hirin
Compensation: £110,000 - £140,000
g forecasts and workforce planning alignment. Lead sourcing strategy for your region, ensuring strong channel performance and diverse talent pools. Monitor funnel health, conversion rates, and time-to-hire metrics. Identify bottlenecks and implement practical process improvements. Activate EVP and attraction initiatives within your market. Support senior or critical hiring projects where required. Drive a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement within TA.________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Key Goals & Objectives: Deliver hiring performance in line with workforce plan and agreed SLAs. Maintain ≤30-day average time-to-hire across supported functions. Maintain 90%+ hiring manager satisfaction within your market. Improve funnel conversion and pipeline strength quarter-on-quarter. Reduce agency reliance through effective sourcing strategy. Ensure consistent ATS discipline and data accuracy across your team. Embed inclusive hiring practices across all roles.Key ResponsibilitiesKey Responsibilities Own market-level recruitment performance and reporting. Lead and develop TA Partners and Resourcing Partners. Facilitate effective intake and workforce planning conversations. Oversee structured interview processes and quality control. Review pipeline data and take action on conversion gaps. Drive sourcing focus across priority and hard-to-fill roles. Partner with Head of TA on forecasting and strategic workforce planning. Represent TA in senior stakeholder discussions. Support onboarding session delivery where required. Champion continuous improvement and operational discipline across TA.Essential Skills & Experience: 5+ years’ recruitment experience (in-house preferred). Experience leading or mentoring recruiters in a fast-paced environment. Strong stakeholder management at senior level. Proven success delivering hiring performance across multiple functions. Strong sourcing understanding and pipeline strategy capability. Comfortable analysing recruitment data and performance metrics. Experience working across multiple markets is advantageous. Confident, structured, and commercially aware. Curious, people-focused, and purpose-driven.

title: Senior Data Engineer
jd_text: We’re partnering with a leading global investment advisory firm to hire a Senior Data Engineer to join their London tech team. This is a fantastic opportunity to work on high-impact data initiatives in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.The Role: As a Senior Data Engineer, you’ll play a key role in designing and building data pipelines from internal systems and external vendors, transforming and modeling data in Snowflake, and working with AI-driven data platforms. You’ll collaborate closely with software engineers, AI teams, and data management, contributing to solutions that have tangible business impact.What You’ll Do:Build and maintain scalable data pipelines and workflowsTransform data in Snowflake, including semantic views and AI agentsWork with internal and external data sources, including APIsApply data engineering best practices to ensure high-quality, testable, maintainable codeParticipate fully in agile development, contributing to a small, high-performing teamWhat We’re Looking For:5+ years of commercial data engineering experience3+ years in Snowflake (including Cortex AI and semantic views)3+ years in Python 3+Strong database querying skills (MySQL preferred)Experience with complex data warehouses, data modeling, and star schemasFamiliarity with APIs and agile methodologiesNice to Have:Azure Data Factory (ADF) experienceExposure to AI tools such as Codex, Claude, or agentic frameworksVisualization experience (Power BI preferred)Experience working with global, cross-functional teams

title: Junior Software Engineer
jd_text:
Position: Junior Software EngineerFull TimeReady for a challenge?Then Just Eat Takeaway.com might be the place for you. We’re a leading global online delivery platform, and our vision is to empower everyday convenience. Whether it’s a Friday-night feast, a post-gym poke bowl, or grabbing some groceries, our tech platform connects tens of millions of customers with hundreds of thousands of restaurant, grocery and convenience partners across the globe.About This RoleWe are looking for a motivated Junior Software Engineer to join our team. In this role, you will contribute to the full lifecycle of our applications, from designing robust backend services to crafting responsive user interfaces. This is a high-growth position perfect for an engineer who is passionate about clean code, scalable architecture, and delivering high-quality features to our customers.These are some of the key components to the position:Build and maintain customer-facing features across the entire stack using Java/Spring Boot and React.Design, develop, and consume REST and GraphQL APIs to ensure seamless data flow.Write efficient queries and manage data structures in MySQL and DynamoDB.Participate in code reviews and write clean, testable code to ensure long-term maintainability.Collaborate with the team to improve system performance, scalability, and web accessibility.What will you bring to the team?Backend & DataBasic to intermediate knowledge of Java development.Interest in or experience with Spring Boot (or similar frameworks).Experience building or consuming APIs and a solid understanding of back
Compensation: £115,000 - £145,000 p.a.
end design principles.Proficiency with MySQL (or other RDBMS) and familiarity with NoSQL concepts like DynamoDB.FrontendBasic knowledge of React and component-based UI development.Familiarity with modern JavaScript or TypeScript.An understanding of state management concepts is a definite plus.Engineering ExcellenceBasic knowledge of Git.Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and automated deployment.A genuine interest in performance, scalability, and the end-user experience.Nice to HavesWhile not required, we’d love to hear from you if you have:Experience with Kotlin.Exposure to Cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure).An interest in using AI tooling to boost development productivity.At JET, This Is On The MenuOur teams forge connections internally and work with some of the best-known brands on the planet, giving us truly international impact in a dynamic environment.Fun, fast-paced and supportive, the JET culture is about movement, growth and about celebrating every aspect of our JETers. Thanks to them we stay one step ahead of the competition.Inclusion, Diversity & BelongingNo matter who you are, what you look like, who you love, or where you are from, you can find your place at Just Eat Takeaway.com. We’re committed to creating an inclusive culture, encouraging diversity of people and thinking, in which all employees feel they truly belong and can bring their most colourful selves to work every day.What else is cooking?Want to know more about our JETers, culture or company? Have a look at our career site where you can find people's stories, blogs, podcasts and more JET morsels.

title: Senior DevOps Engineer - AWS Infrastructure Architect
jd_text:
About DeliveryAppDeliveryApp is in an exciting phase of rapid growth. As we scale our operations and technology, we are expanding our engineering team to build the next generation of our platform. We are looking for a highly capable Senior DevOps Engineer to step in and take full ownership of our cloud infrastructure. You will not just be managing servers; you will be driving the architecture, making critical technical decisions, and leading the implementation of our DevOps capabilities from the ground up.The Mission You will be architecting highly reliable, scalable, and secure AWS infrastructure designed to support high-impact, real-world logistics operations. Expect to tackle complex challenges involving high-availability architecture, seamless deployment pipelines, and proactive disaster recovery to keep our core network running flawlessly.The Role This role owns infrastructure reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency. We are looking for an engineer with deep Infrastructure-as-Code maturity who will take complete ownership of designing, deploying, and maintaining sophisticated AWS environments that drive real value for the business.
The salary for this role is £95,000.
Core StackCloud: AWS (EC2, EKS, RDS, Lambda, S3, CloudFront)Infrastructure-as-Code: Terraform / CloudFormationContainerization: Docker / KubernetesCI/CD: GitHub Actions, JenkinsObservability: CloudWatch, Prometheus, GrafanaWhat Sets This Role ApartSecurity: A strong, security-first mindsetFinOps: Advanced cost optimization strategies and FinOps awarenessResilience: Deep expertise in disaster recovery and high-availability architectureAutomation: High maturity in Infrastructure-as-Code practicesRequirementsExperience: 5+ years of dedicated DevOps experienceScale Management: Proven track record managing production-scale AWS infrastructureIncident Response: Strong troubleshooting and resolution skills during live incidentsCommunication: Excellent and fluent English speaking skills are absolutely essential. You must have the ability to communicate clearly and effectively across both engineering and product teamsWorking Model: A minimum of 4 days a week on-site is required for deep collaboration, strategic planning, and essential on-site presence during major deployments or outagesCompensationSalary: Competitive and negotiable based on experience

title: Senior Software Engineer
jd_text: Staff Product Engineer - £150,000 + Equity Location: Shoreditch, LondonAbout the OpportunityA high-growth AI-first law firm is looking to transform the UK conveyancing market, starting with the legal process behind every property transaction. Currently, conveyancing is slow, opaque and unreliable, with one in three transactions falling through and complaints significantly higher than in other legal sectors. Many firms still rely on outdated manual processes.This business aims to disrupt a £2.5 billion market by combining deep domain expertise with cutting-edge AI technology, with ambitions to expand into a full-service, AI-powered law firm with global reach.The RoleThe firm is seeking a Staff Product Engineer to join as an early member of a small, high-calibre engineering team.This is a product-first role: from day one, the engineer will help shape product direction, speak directly with users, define ambiguous requirements and ship features to production quickly. The team values engineers who take ownership of outcomes and think beyond tickets.Candidate ProfileExperience in early-stage startups, influencing product strategyUser-problem focused, not purely technical solution-orientedExperience scaling TypeScript codebasesComfortable building AI agents or LLM-powered systems beyond simple promptingHigh attention to detail and pride in engineering craftComfortable operating in ambiguity and moving quicklyBonus points for:Academic or applied experience in machine learningExperience bridging research and production systemsWhat’s on Offer£150,000 depending on experienceMeaningful early-stage equity5% employer pension contributionComprehensive private medical cover including dental and optical25 days holiday plus bank holidaysChoice of AI tooling

title: Senior ML Engineer / ML Lead
jd_text: Your missionTo build and lead Track Titan's ML capability at a pivotal moment for our AI direction. You will take our unique dataset – millions of laps of racing telemetry across thousands of car/track combinations – and turn it into the foundation of a genuinely new kind of AI coaching platform. You'll be hands-on technically from day one, while growing and guiding a small team that will define how we do ML at Track Titan.What you will do:Build the telemetry understanding layer. Design and train the models that learn meaningful representations from racing telemetry – self-supervised encoders, sequence models, embedding systems. This is the technical foundation everything else builds on, and you'll own it end-to-end: from problem formulation through architecture decisions to production deployment.Evolve our coaching intelligence. We have an existing coaching system and a massive dataset – your job is to unlock the next level. You'll introduce new approaches to problems like mistake identification and time-loss attribution that scale with our data, moving us from domain-engineered solutions toward learned models that generalise across cars, tracks, and driving styles.Set the ML technical direction. Evaluate approaches, define the model and data strategy, and make the architectural decisions that will shape our ML stack for years. Establish the practices that make ML work reliable – experiment tracking, evaluation harnesses, reproducibility. You'll work closely with the CTPO (who has deep AI/ML experience) to pressure-test ideas and align ML work with product and business priorities. You'll have strong opinions, the technical depth to back them up, and the humility to change your mind when the data says otherwise.Lead and grow the ML team. You'll lead a small, growing ML team and be responsible for raising the bar: code quality, experimentation rigour, review culture, and technical development. As we scale, you'll shape what the ML function looks like and play a key role in hiring the people who join it.Ship models that reach users. Research taste matters, but so does pragmatism. You'll balance long-term foundational work with near-term product impact, finding the stepping stones where better models directly improve insights and coaching quality for hundreds of thousands of drivers. You'll define clear contracts with the product engineering team and own ML systems through to production.Your profile5+ years of hands-on ML engineering experience, with meaningful time spent on self-supervised learning, representation learning, or sequence modelling. You've trained models on real-world data at scale – not just reproduced papers or done abstract research.Deep experience with modern sequence modelling. You've worked with transformers on sequential data – NLP, time-series, sensor data, trajectories, audio, or similar. You understand the design choices (tokenisation, patching, positional encoding, multi-scale architectures) and have opinions about when they matter and when they don't.Strong embedding and retrieval intuitions. You've built systems where learned representations are the product – similarity search, clustering, retrieval, recommendation. You understand contrastive learning, evaluation of embedding quality, and the practical challenges of making representations useful downstream.Production ML experience. You've taken models from research to production and dealt with the realities: data quality, pipeline reliability, monitoring, and the gap between offline metrics and real-world performance. You don't need a separate team to productionise your work.Technical leadership experience or clear readiness for it. You've mentored engineers, led technical direction for a team or project, or set standards that others follow. You don't need to have managed a team formally, but you should be ready to – and excited about raising the level of the people around you.A cross-domain thinker. We're building something genuinely new, and we want someone who gets energised by that, who can draw on ideas from diverse fields like NLP, audio, robotics, or control theory and apply them with fresh eyes. If you've worked across multiple domains or brought techniques from one field to another, that's exactly the profile.Excellent written communicator. As a partially distributed, async-first team, writing is how we think together. Technical proposals, experiment write-ups, architectural decisions – you express complex ideas clearly and concisely, and you engage constructively with others' writing.Motorsport passion or experience is a strong plus – but not a firm requirement. Some of our team are professional racing drivers. Others came in as fans or curious newcomers and went deep. What matters is genuine curiosity and a willingness to learn. If you join, you'll be expected to immerse yourself in this world and grow your understanding on things like vehicle dynamics. But we don’t expect you to be an expert on this (we have a lot of existing domain expertise on the team that you can tap into).A research background (PhD or equivalent depth) is valued but not required. What matters is the depth of your technical intuition and your ability to formulate problems well – qualifications are secondary.Fluent with AI tools in your daily workflow. You already use LLMs and AI-powered tools to move faster – whether for research, writing, analysis, or prototyping. We're an AI company and we expect everyone to work like one.What this role is not:A pure research position – you'll publish if it makes sense, but the goal is product impactA prompt engineering role – we mean real modelling, not LLM wranglingAn MLOps or platform engineering role – you'll care about infrastructure, but your focus is modelling and representation learningPurely executing someone else's technical vision – you'll shape the direction, working closely with a technically deep CTPO who will both challenge and support youWhy us?A genuinely unprecedented ML challenge. Millions of laps. Thousands of car/track combinations. Multivariate time-series with causal structure, cross-domain transfer problems, and a direct line from better representations to better coaching. If you've been looking for a dataset and problem that nobody else has, this is it.Build the ML function from the ground up. We have a small foundation, but you won't inherit a large legacy system you can't change or a team culture you didn't shape. You'll define how we do ML – the architecture, the practices, the team – at a moment where those decisions compound.Work with leadership that speaks your language. Our CTPO has deep AI/ML experience (previously led Applied AI at a public tech company) and will be a genuine technical partner – not someone you need to translate for. We also have a team that’s extremely deep and knowledgable in the motorsport domain.Flexible location. We have a great London HQ, and while we would love to have you in London, we are flexible (and our existing team is distributed). Europe is generally no problem, and for exceptional candidates global remote could also be negotiable on a case-by-case basis for this role.Join the only motorsport startup in the world backed by tier-one VCsWork on cutting-edge tech, campaigns, and partnerships that reach users across the globe 24/7Join a team of fellow motorsport fans and sim racers who have backgrounds in top startups, sports data science, tier-one consulting, and professional motorsportQuarterly learning day and budgetGenerous options package as a key early employeePrivate pension (for UK employees)Private health insurance (for UK employees)About UsWant to build the "Strava for Motorsport"?At Track Titan, we are building the “Strava for Motorsport” by combining AI driver coaching with community and social features.With a user-base in the hundreds of thousands and millions of laps processed, Track Titan is already the dominant simracing coaching platform and we have big plans to go further. We are a team of fifteen backed by some of the world’s best VCs, strategic investors such as Porsche and have recently launched integrations with key industry players such as MOZA and Fanatec. This is your one-time chance to build a venture-scale startup in the most exciting sport on the planet.

You'll lead a small, growing ML team and be responsible for raising the bar: code quality, experimentation rigour, review culture, and technical development. As we scale, you'll shape what the ML function looks like and play a key role in hiring the people who join it.Ship models that reach users. Research taste matters, but so does pragmatism. You'll balance long-term foundational work with near-term product impact, finding the stepping stones where better models directly improve insights and coaching quality for hundreds of thousands of drivers. You'll define clear contracts with the product engineering team and own ML systems through to production.Your profile5+ years of hands-on ML engineering experience, with meaningful time spent on self-supervised learning, representation learning, or sequence modelling. You've trained models on real-world data at scale – not just reproduced papers or done abstract research.Deep experience with modern sequence modelling. You've worked with transformers on sequential data – NLP, time-series, sensor data, trajectories, audio, or similar. You understand the design choices (tokenisation, patching, positional encoding, multi-scale architectures) and have opinions about when they matter and when they don't.Strong embedding and retrieval intuitions. You've built systems where learned representations are the product – similarity search, clustering, retrieval, recommendation. You understand contrastive learning, evaluation of embedding quality, and the practical challenges of making representations useful downstream.Production ML experience. You've taken models from research to production and dealt with the realities: data quality, pipeline reliability, monitoring, and the gap between offline metrics and real-world performance. You don't need a separate team to productionise your work.Technical leadership experience or clear readiness for it. You've mentored engineers, led technical direction for a team or project, or set standards that others follow. You don't need to have managed a team formally, but you should be ready to – and excited about raising the level of the people around you.A cross-domain thinker. We're building something genuinely new, and we want someone who gets energised by that, who can draw on ideas from diverse fields like NLP, audio, robotics, or control theory and apply them with fresh eyes. If you've worked across multiple domains or brought techniques from one field to another, that's exactly the profile.Excellent written communicator. As a partially distributed, async-first team, writing is how we think together. Technical proposals, experiment write-ups, architectural decisions – you express complex ideas clearly and concisely, and you engage constructively with others' writing.Motorsport passion or experience is a strong plus – but not a firm requirement. Some of our team are professional racing drivers. Others came in as fans or curious newcomers and went deep. What matters is genuine curiosity and a willingness to learn. If you join, you'll be expected to immerse yourself in this world and grow your understanding on things like vehicle dynamics. But we don’t expect you to be an expert on this (we have a lot of existing domain expertise on the team that you can tap into).A research background (PhD or equivalent depth) is valued but not required. What matters is the depth of your technical intuition and your ability to formulate problems well – qualifications are secondary.Fluent with AI tools in your daily workflow. You already use LLMs and AI-powered tools to move faster – whether for research, writing, analysis, or prototyping. We're an AI company and we expect everyone to work like one.What this role is not:A pure research position – you'll publish if it makes sense, but the goal is product impactA prompt engineering role – we mean real modelling, not LLM wranglingAn MLOps or platform engineering role – you'll care about infrastructure, but your focus is modelling and representation learningPurely executing someone else's technical vision – you'll shape the direction, working closely with a technically deep CTPO who will both challenge and support youWhy us?A genuinely unprecedented ML challenge. Millions of laps. Thousands of car/track combinations. Multivariate time-series with causal structure, cross-domain transfer problems, and a direct line from better representations to better coaching. If you've been looking for a dataset and problem that nobody else has, this is it.Build the ML function from the ground up. We have a small foundation, but you won't inherit a large legacy system you can't change or a team culture you didn't shape. You'll define how we do ML – the architecture, the practices, the team – at a moment where those decisions compound.Work with leadership that speaks your language. Our CTPO has deep AI/ML experience (previously led Applied AI at a public tech company) and will be a genuine technical partner – not someone you need to translate for. We also have a team that’s extremely deep and knowledgable in the motorsport domain.Flexible location. We have a great London HQ, and while we would love to have you in London, we are flexible (and our existing team is distributed). Europe is generally no problem, and for exceptional candidates global remote could also be negotiable on a case-by-case basis for this role.Join the only motorsport startup in the world backed by tier-one VCsWork on cutting-edge tech, campaigns, and partnerships that reach users across the globe 24/7Join a team of fellow motorsport fans and sim racers who have backgrounds in top startups, sports data science, tier-one consulting, and professional motorsportQuarterly learning day and budgetGenerous options package as a key early employeePrivate pension (for UK employees)Private health insurance (for UK employees)About UsWant to build the "Strava for Motorsport"?At Track Titan, we are building the “Strava for Motorsport” by combining AI driver coaching with community and social features.With a user-base in the hundreds of thousands and millions of laps processed, Track Titan is already the dominant simracing coaching platform and we have big plans to go further. We are a team of fifteen backed by some of the world’s best VCs, strategic investors such as Porsche and have recently launched integrations with key industry players such as MOZA and Fanatec. This is your one-time chance to build a venture-scale startup in the most exciting sport on the planet.

title: Senior Software Engineer
jd_text: Work with Us. Change the World.At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world’s most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations.There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of over 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world.Job DescriptionIn AECOM’s AI Engineering team your code will directly shape the physical world around us. We build AI-driven technology that revolutionises how infrastructure and buildings are designed and engineered; reducing waste, cutting CO₂, and making the built environment more efficient and sustainable. This is where software has measurable, real-world impact.With our AI Engineering team we’ve created a unique setup: a lean, highly technical team with the speed and ownership of a start up, backed by the scale, resources, and domain expertise of one of the world’s leading engineering firms.There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. We are leading the industry’s AI transformation, and with our people and technology we deliver excellence and innovate with impact.We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams to solve the world’s most complex challenges. We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us.What You’ll DoAs a Senior Software Engineer, you’ll work at the intersection of software engineering and machine learning operations.This role is intentionally broad. We’re hiring multiple engineers with complementary strengths across platform engineering and ML systems. No single person is expected to cover everything. Depth in one area, strong fundamentals, and the ability to collaborate effectively matter more than perfect overlap.Part of the work focuses on core platform engineering: designing and building services, evolving system architecture, improving reliability, resolving issues, and making sound trade-offs in areas such as database design and schema evolution.Another part focuses on ML systems: ensuring models run reliably, efficiently, and at scale, turning research into production-ready tools. This includes deploying, monitoring, and optimizing AI workloads using Python and solid software engineering practices.QualificationsMust-Have QualificationsBachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field (or equivalent experience). Solid programming skills in Python (plus experience with frameworks such as FastAPI, Flask, or Django). Hands-on experience with ML model deployment and monitoring in production. Knowledge of containerization (Docker). Experience building CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions). Experience working within startups / scaleups. Strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset. Knowledge of Agile delivery models (Scrum/Kanban) and software development best practices. 3+ years of hands-on experience building production-grade systems. Preferred SkillsUnderstanding of optimizing both CPU-bound and GPU-bound workloads. Knowledge of monitoring and observability tools (e.g. Prometheus, Grafana, ELK stack). Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform). Experience with Azure. Identifying and resolving bottlenecks in distributed machine learning workloads (knowledge of low-level languages and CUDA library is a plus). Experience in building design automation products within the construction industry. Additional InformationOur Hiring Process25-minute screening call. Coding challenge: A hands-on task to assess your problem-solving and technical skills. Final cultural fit: In-person meeting with our leadership team. Why Join Us?Work on real-world problems where AI creates measurable impact. Be part of a team where your work matters, and your ideas become real. Collaborate with sharp, driven colleagues in a culture of trust, ownership, and high standards. Contribute to making the built environment smarter and more sustainable. About AECOMAECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public- and private-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16.1 billion in fiscal year 2025. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM.Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed.You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients.AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too.Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours.We are a Disability Confident Employer and will offer an interview to applicants who have a disability or long-term condition, who meet the minimum/essential criteria for the role. Please let us know using this email address ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com if you would like to apply through the Disability Confident Interview Scheme.All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Senior Electrical Design Engineer
jd_text:
We offer a salary of 80k–95k.
Senior Electrical Design EngineerDynisma is revolutionising the automotive and motorsport driving simulator market with its disruptive Motion Generator technology. Our cutting-edge approach to simulator design delivers more data about vehicle dynamics and ride to drivers and passengers with less latency and more accurately than any other motion simulators available.About the team:We are a team of engineers experienced both in the design and manufacture of driving simulators, as well as their use in vehicle development. We develop, deliver and support outstanding, ground-breaking simulators and motion generators that support the engineering that leads to winning performance on the track.The role:Dynisma is redefining the design, build and operation of motion simulation technology, with highly disruptive products. Our technology targets the changing and expanding global markets for motion simulation including motorsport, automotive, defence and aviation.We have world leading technical knowledge and are now searching for an outstanding Senior Electrical Design Engineer to work on the new model of motion generator which will be placed on the non-industial market.ResponsibilitiesInterpret requirements and produce design specifications.Produce technical documentation to support designs.Provide technical/functional input at all stages from concept through to in-service support.Specify electrical control panels and systems designs for subcontract design and manufacture.Create, review, and approve electrical documentation (schematics, schedules, calculations).Create and manage BoM data for electrical hardware.Be on company site for integration and commissioning of electrical power and control systems.Ensure engineering outputs meets quality and regulatory standards (EN60335; EN60204; ISO12100; EMC; EN13849; NFPA79; UL508A)Support cross-functional collaboration and continuous improvement activitiesSupport field installations in the UK but also globallyProvide in-service support (maintenance/upgrades/ modifications)QualificationsEssential:3-4 years’ experience in a relevant field of work (New product development/introduction)BSc, BEng, MSc, MEng in Electrical Engineering or other relevant field of studyDesired:Member of a professional institute/organisationSkills and ExperienceExperience with the specification of electrical control panels.Experience in using an electrical CAD softwareExperience in using AutoCAD or Eplan.Strong organisational and communication skillsAbility to manage various tasks simultaneouslyHands-on experience to support fault finding and building activities when requiredWhy join Dynisma?Dynisma is growing rapidly and we are looking for outstanding people across all parts of the business to help build our future.The culture is passionate, fast-paced, open and transparent. People work in a flat structure with hands-on application, innovation and experimentation constantly in mind.Benefits include a competitive basic salary, pension, life insurance, private medical insurance, 25 days holiday, Cycle to work and electric car vehicle schemes.If this sounds exciting and you want to join a seriously high performing team, determined to always deliver excellence, then please apply to recruitment@dynisma.com.DYNISMA IS PROUD TO BE AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYERWe are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where everyone feels valued and respected.We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other legally protected status.If you require any special assistance or accommodations during the recruitment or interview process, please let us know. We are happy to support you to ensure a fair and comfortable experience.

Our technology targets the changing and expanding global markets for motion simulation including motorsport, automotive, defence and aviation.We have world leading technical knowledge and are now searching for an outstanding Senior Electrical Design Engineer to work on the new model of motion generator which will be placed on the non-industial market.ResponsibilitiesInterpret requirements and produce design specifications.Produce technical documentation to support designs.Provide technical/functional input at all stages from concept through to in-service support.Specify electrical control panels and systems designs for subcontract design and manufacture.Create, review, and approve electrical documentation (schematics, schedules, calculations).Create and manage BoM data for electrical hardware.Be on company site for integration and commissioning of electrical power and control systems.Ensure engineering outputs meets quality and regulatory standards (EN60335; EN60204; ISO12100; EMC; EN13849; NFPA79; UL508A)Support cross-functional collaboration and continuous im
Compensation: £110,000 - £140,000 DOE
provement activitiesSupport field installations in the UK but also globallyProvide in-service support (maintenance/upgrades/ modifications)QualificationsEssential:3-4 years’ experience in a relevant field of work (New product development/introduction)BSc, BEng, MSc, MEng in Electrical Engineering or other relevant field of studyDesired:Member of a professional institute/organisationSkills and ExperienceExperience with the specification of electrical control panels.Experience in using an electrical CAD softwareExperience in using AutoCAD or Eplan.Strong organisational and communication skillsAbility to manage various tasks simultaneouslyHands-on experience to support fault finding and building activities when requiredWhy join Dynisma?Dynisma is growing rapidly and we are looking for outstanding people across all parts of the business to help build our future.The culture is passionate, fast-paced, open and transparent. People work in a flat structure with hands-on application, innovation and experimentation constantly in mind.Benefits include a competitive basic salary, pension, life insurance, private medical insurance, 25 days holiday, Cycle to work and electric car vehicle schemes.If this sounds exciting and you want to join a seriously high performing team, determined to always deliver excellence, then please apply to recruitment@dynisma.com.DYNISMA IS PROUD TO BE AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYERWe are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where everyone feels valued and respected.We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other legally protected status.If you require any special assistance or accommodations during the recruitment or interview process, please let us know. We are happy to support you to ensure a fair and comfortable experience.

title: Software Engineer
jd_text:
Compensation: From £115,000
Software EngineerLocation: Newcastle (60% onsite)Contract: 12 Months with strong likelihood of extensionHours: 35 per week (4.5‑day week between 7am–7pm, business discretion)IR35: Inside of IR35 Pay: £52.32 p/h PAYE / £70 p/h UmbrellaOur Client, a leader in advanced technology solutions within the Defence & Space sector, is seeking a skilled Software Engineer to join their innovative and collaborative Agile Scrum team. Working with cutting‑edge cloud technologies and geospatial software, you will contribute to every stage of the development lifecycle—from design through to deployment.Key ResponsibilitiesDevelop high‑quality, readable, and maintainable code using Java and modern cloud-based technologies.Create scalable and reusable software patterns to support ongoing development velocity.Participate in production of unit and automated tests to ensure robust software delivery.Conduct and contribute to design and code reviews, ensuring adherence to best practices, security standards, and maintainability guidelines.Document developed solutions and share technical knowledge with the wider team.Actively contribute to Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, daily stand-ups, and sprint reviews.Work collaboratively with the Scrum team to meet sprint objectives and delivery commitments.Maintain awareness of compliance risks and uphold integrity in all work activities.Essential SkillsStrong experience with JavaCloud services developmentUnit testing and automated testing practicesDesirable SkillsDevSecOps tools and pipelines (e.g., Tekton, ArgoCD, SonarQube)Experience with Red Hat OpenShift or KubernetesExperience using the Quarkus frameworkFront‑end development skills (e.g., Angular)Given the role's nature, the selected candidate will undergo BPSS clearance before commencing and will subsequently obtain SC clearance. If you think this could be a new and exciting career opportunity for you and you would like to learn more or just for an initial, no-obligation conversation please contact me on 07732 694 243 or you can apply directly by following the Easy Apply link and I will contact you as soon as I receive your contact details.

title: Data Engineer
jd_text:
About UsAt Environment Bank, we’re working together to help restore natural habitats across England. With thousands of acres of new habitat creation already underway, our projects are delivering biodiversity gains and helping vital ecosystems to recover and flourish.Our diverse team includes ecologists, land managers, habitat specialists, geospatial analysts, planning advisors, account managers, digital creatives, legal experts, and more.From our habitat specialists to our sales and marketing teams, everyone at Environment Bank is a champion for our values, working towards our goal of reversing biodiversity loss.Environment Bank is a market-leading organisation in a sector that’s experiencing rapid growth. We’ve already built a highly skilled team of experts and we’re actively looking to expand our team in the coming months.We’re incredibly proud of all we achieved so far and we’re still growing. If you'd like to be part of a driven organisation that's making a real difference, find your next role with the team at Environment Bank.About The RoleThe Data Engineer will play a crucial role in helping the Environment Bank meet its strategic objectives around integration and data reporting. This position is development focused, working closely with teams across the business to streamline data pipelines and improve task efficiency. This individual will focus on setting up automated ETL workflows, analysing data and producing visually appealing and user-friendly reports, and supporting internal and external stakeholders fulfil their data requirements.ResponsibilitiesWork collaboratively on the development of our central cloud-based data platform (Snowflake) Develop and maintain ETL and data integration tools for data flows and pipelines Create and maintain reports and dashboards for a variety of audiences around the business, and the underlying data platform environment Innovate and streamline existing data integrations and processes by identifying gaps and improvements, and designing solutions Mentor and upskill team members Provide data and platform related technical support across the business Create, maintain, and update documentation EDI & BelongingAt the heart of Environment Bank are the people, who make it all possible. We employ individuals, who share the same passion to live our Environment Bank mission and values.At Environment Bank we believe that fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace makes us more innovative, dynamic, and competitive. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, ethnicities, cultures, and experiences. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone is valued and respected, free from discrimination based on race, religion, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, gender identity or expression, or any other legally protected status.RequirementsEssentialSelf-starter with curious mindset, a desire to help colleagues through problem solving and learning new skills Desire to be part of a team supporting our nature recovery work Experience with programming languages (Python, SQL) maintaining, creating or integrating APIs and data modelling Experience with ETL and data platform integrating (Microsoft Power Platform, Azure, APIs, custom code integrations) Experience with Snowflake cloud data platform and the development environment Experience visualising data using dashboards to communicate to a variety of audiences (e.g. Streamlit) Strong understanding of data management, version control (GitHub) and quality assurance/ quality control Strong attention to detail, time management and organisational skills Ability to work collaboratively and independently as part of a fully remote team Ability to manage multiple tasks and projects in fast-paced environment Bachelor’s degree in a relevant or related field (e.g. Computer Science, Data Science, Maths etc.), or equivalent professional experience or qualification DesirableExperience within the ecology or environmental sector, with an awareness of Biodiversity Net Gain and nature restoration Understanding of spatial data integration Experience with our other wider systems architecture (e.g. HubSpot, Asana, Azure, and others) BenefitsAs an organisation, we believe that work should be fulfilling, inspiring, and balanced, so we’re creating a space where our team can thrive, both personally and professionally – providing flexible working arrangements, mental wellbeing support, and more.We offer...Performance-based annual bonus up to 10% of salaryPension scheme with employer-matched contributions at 6% of salary️ 30 days of annual leave, plus paid bank holidays, birthday, and wellbeing day➕ Option to purchase 5 additional days of annual leave after probationRemote and hybrid flexible working optionsRegional and departmental team co-working daysExpenses-paid Whole Company Meet Twice a Year️ Team fundraising and charity eventsOngoing career development with training and mentoringMedical cash back scheme to cover everyday healthcare costs‍ Employee assistance mental wellbeing support❤️‍️ Enhanced sickness pay allowance
Salary: £145,000

title: Data Scientist
jd_text: Senior Data Scientist | London | HybridWe're working with a global leader in entertainment and communications, the powerhouse behind some of the world's most loved streaming services and broadcasting hardware, on this exciting opportunity.This is a chance to join a high-impact Group AI function, building next-generation Large Language Model (LLM) solutions that define the future of customer experience. You’ll be at the forefront of Generative AI, leveraging massive datasets to move beyond experimentation into production-grade RAG systems and conversational agents.The Role• Lead the design and development of Generative AI solutions, including Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), conversational AI, and synthetic content creation.• Translate complex business challenges into structured Data Science problems, defining hypotheses and measurable success metrics for the business.• Build and implement rigorous LLM evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-a-judge) and statistical significance testing to validate model performance.• Collaborate with cross-functional teams of Engineers and Product owners to take AI-driven products from initial ideation to full-scale deployment.• Leverage immense proprietary data assets to establish robust evaluation benchmarks and scalable AI solutions.What You'll Need• Advanced degree (MSc or PhD) in a quantitative field (Statistics, Physics, Maths, CS) or equivalent deep industry experience in Data Science.• Hands-on experience developing and deploying Large Language Model (LLM) applications within a large-scale enterprise environment.• Expert-level Python skills and deep familiarity with the ML ecosystem, including PyTorch, Scikit-learn, and XGBoost.• Proven experience with Vector Databases, data querying languages, and advanced information retrieval methods.• A commercial mindset with the ability to communicate complex technical concepts to stakeholders in terms of business value and ROI.What's On Offer• Highly competitive benefits including premium TV and streaming packages at exclusive rates.• Comprehensive private healthcare and a generous pension contribution package.• Access to a world-class campus featuring onsite cinemas, subsidised gyms, restaurants, and beauty salons.• Flexible hybrid working model balancing home convenience with unique, collaborative office spaces.Apply via Haystack today!

title: Front End Developer (remote)
jd_text:
If you’ve used an App to order food or pay a bill in a restaurant, you may have been one of the millions of users who use our Apps for ordering, paying bills, and earning loyalty rewards. We’re a London-base company building the leading digital hospitality platform, and that means designing and building iOS and Android Apps, management web portals, backend services, e-pos integrations and much more.We have an immediate vacancy for a junior/mid level front-end developer to join our team and help us build best-in-class web applications.The RoleThe roadmap for our web app and our portal web site has many ambitious features; they will be a challenge to implement.This is a hands-on role developing web applications using React.js and Typescript. You’ll be working with your colleagues in the Front End team and the Platform team to architect new features, and build performant and delightful user experiences.We use an agile Kanban process for development. We emphasise planning and design in our process, and aim for fast, continuous delivery.Who Should ApplyWe are looking for developers with 1-3 years’ experience in a commercial team. You’ve worked on real-world websites or applications, know how to problem solve, and you set a high quality standard.In particular we are looking for:experience with React.js and related design patternsexperience in ES6, Typescript and Debugging, Javascript build tools and packagersexperience in web service technologies and knowledge of Node.jsFluent spoken EnglishA university degree in CS, or STEM subjectWhile we embrace the advantages of AI in our workflow, it is expected that interviews will be carried out without the use of such tools.LocationThis role is suitable for remote working. In fact all our developers work remotely, but you do have the option to work in the office if you want to and if you are based in the UK. You will always find Pepper staff in our London office at Shepherds Bush.Job Benefits & PerksRemote / Home Working, or London WeWork optionCompetitive Salary25 Days Holiday + Bank Holiday (rising to 28 days with service ) Stock Options (on promotion)Modern Cloud TechWork on Customer Facing SystemsExperienced Co-Workers Development Best PracticesCareer Advancement OpportunitiesClaude Subscription
Salary: £130k

title: Senior Java Developer
jd_text: JOB TITLE: Senior Java DeveloperSALARY: £70,929 - £86,691 per annumLOCATION(S): BristolHOURS: Full-time - 35 hours per week WORKING PATTERN: Our work style is hybrid, which involves spending at least two days per week, or 40% of our time, at one of our offices stated above. ABOUT THIS OPPORTUNITYWe're not just a bank. We're a technology-led organisation with a bold ambition: to shape finance as a force for good. As we continue to invest in our people, platforms, and products, we're looking for passionate Senior Java Developers to help us create secure, scalable, and customer-first solutions. If you're someone who thrives in collaborative environments, enjoys solving meaningful problems, and wants to make a real impact - this could be the perfect next step in your career. WHAT YOU'LL BE DOINGAs a senior software engineer, you'll play a key role in designing and building modern applications that touch millions of lives. You'll:Design, develop, test, and deploy backend services.Build and deploy containerized applications with Docker and Kubernetes.Apply Domain-Driven Design (DDD) and Test-Driven Development (TDD) principles.Collaborate closely with Product Owners, Architects, and Quality Engineers.Integrate with internal systems and third-party APIs to create seamless user experiences.Contribute to architectural decisions and help shape our engineering strategy.Mentor junior engineers and foster a culture of learning and inclusion. WHAT YOU'LL NEEDWe're looking for engineers who are adaptable, curious, and passionate about building great software! To help us understand how you might contribute to our team, please highlight any relevant experience with the following skills and technologies in your application:Backend DevelopmentProven hands-on experience with Java, including designing and implementing RESTful APIs that support scalable, secure, and high-performance architectures.Familiarity with backend frameworks such as Spring Boot and Node.js is beneficial. DevOps & CloudExperience with CI/CD tools such as GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or Harness.Familiarity with Docker for containerization and Kubernetes for orchestration.Exposure to cloud platforms - Google Cloud Platform (GCP) preferred, but AWS or Azure experience is also welcome. Quality Testing & AutomationExperience with automated testing tools like Jest, Cypress, or Pact.Any experience with Junit, Mokito, PowerMockCommitment to writing reliable, maintainable, and well-tested code. If you're selected to move forward in our assessment process, you may be invited to complete a technical interview with Karat - our trusted interviewing partner. As part of this stage, we'll share your information with Karat, in line with their privacy policy. We're committed to making our recruitment process inclusive and accessible. If you need any adjustments, just let us know - we're here to support you. We're proud to be a leader in diversity, equity, and inclusion. From our menopause health package to our Working with Cancer initiative, we're creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. We're also a Disability Confident Leader, and we guarantee interviews for a fair and proportionate number of applicants with disabilities, long-term health conditions, or neurodivergence who meet the minimum criteria.Our benefits include:Up to 15% pension contributionAnnual bonus (based on Group performance)Share schemes, including free shares30 days' holiday plus bank holidaysFlexible benefits tailored to your lifestyleGenerous parental leave and wellbeing support Ready for a career where you can have a positive impact as you learn, grow and thrive? Apply today and find out more!

title: Senior Site Reliability Engineer
jd_text: JOB TITLE: Senior Site Reliability EngineerSALARY: £72,702 - £80,780LOCATION(S): BristolHOURS: Full-time - 35 hours per weekWORKING PATTERN: Our work style is hybrid, which involves spending at least two days per week, or 40% of our time, at our Bristol office. About this opportunityOur SRE (Site Reliability Engineering) team within the Analytics & AI platform are looking for an experienced and passionate Engineer with strong hands-on development experience. As an SRE you'll be working with a team of engineers on a suite of automation and gen AI products. You'll run and maintain a set of applications and services on a combination of Private and Public Clouds that will enable the business to realise the next generation of services that form the Bank's vision for 2026 and beyond! What you'll do Play a role in supporting a multi-application, multi-platform teamInvestigate, fix and remediate service issues to improve the performance and the reliability of our diverse application estateContribute to and/or lead post incident reviews and drive problem resolution to prevent recurrenceIdentify and eliminate toil through automation with a focus on continuous improvementCreate pro-active monitoring and observability solutions to help us see issues before our customers doDefine and measure Service Level Objectives and Service Level IndicatorsLead initiatives within the team, and possible Line Management of colleagues Why Lloyds Banking GroupWe're on an exciting journey to transform our Group and the way we're shaping finance for good. We're focusing on the future, investing in our technologies, workplaces, and colleagues to make our Group a great place for everyone. Including you! What you'll needStrong practitioner in SRE principles (SLI, SLO & SLA) using Observability, Logging, Monitoring & AlertingStrong software engineering capability, including hands-on development experience building, testing and maintaining production applications.Can independently design, implement, manage and optimise cloud deployments, proficient in using orchestrations tools such as Jenkins, GitHub, and HarnessStrong DevOps understanding, including experience of Infrastructure as Code and CI/CD pipelines, such as Terraform and Jenkins, or alternatives such as Azure DevOpsConfident programming and troubleshooting issues in at least one of the following languages: Java, Groovy, Python, Ruby, JavaScript, PowerShell or Bash/Shell. Experience of developing for, or administrating KubernetesExperience in identifying toil and building automated solutions to remove itExperience working with a broad set of GCP products (or extensive experience with another Public Cloud platform, such as Azure or AWS) and Cloud security and networking It would be great if you also hadExperience in the banking and finance industryUsed Jira, Confluence and understood Agile working practicesCertifications in Azure or another cloud platform, such as GCP or AWSExperience working in an SRE role or you may currently be working in a software engineering role or DevOps role and would like to explore a move into SREExperience working with CTWO, Elastic Stack (ELK), and Splunk. About working for usOur ambition is to be the leading UK business for diversity, equity and inclusion supporting our customers, colleagues and communities and we're committed to creating an environment in which everyone can thrive, learn and develop. We were one of the first major organisations to set goals on diversity in senior roles, create a menopause health package, and a dedicated Working with Cancer Initiative. We offer reasonable workplace adjustments for colleagues with disabilities, including flexibility in office attendance, location and working patterns. And, as a Disability Confident Leader, we guarantee interviews for a fair and proportionate number of applicants who meet the minimum criteria for the role with a disability, long-term health or neurodivergent condition through the Disability Confident Scheme. We provide reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process to reduce or remove barriers. Just let us know what you need. We also offer a wide-ranging benefits package, which includes:A generous pension contribution of up to 15%An annual performance-related bonusShare schemes including free sharesBenefits you can adapt to your lifestyle, such as discounted shopping30 days' holiday, with bank holidays on topA range of wellbeing initiatives and generous parental leave policies Our continued commitment to helping Britain prosper means that as a colleague you can make a difference to customers, businesses and communities. Together we have a key role to play in shaping the bank of the future, whilst the scale and reach of our Group means you'll continue to have opportunities to learn, grow and develop. We're focused on creating a values-led culture, and our approach to inclusion and diversity means that we all have the opportunity to make a real difference, together. As part of the Group's commitments as a result of ring-fencing legislation, colleagues based in the Islands are required to be exclusively dedicated to the non-ring-fenced bank and its subsidiaries. This means that colleagues who are based in the Islands would not be able to undertake roles for the Ring Fenced Bank from their existing location and would need to consider relocation when applying for roles.

We also offer a wide-ranging benefits package, which includes:A generous pension contribution of up to 15%An annual performance-related bonusShare schemes including free sharesBenefits you can adapt to your lifestyle, such as discounted shopping30 days' holiday, with bank holidays on topA range of wellbeing initiatives and generous parental leave policies Our continued commitment to helping Britain prosper means that as a colleague you can make a difference to customers, businesses and communities. Together we have a key role to play in shaping the bank of the future, whilst the scale and reach of our Group means you'll continue to have opportunities to learn, grow and develop. We're focused on creating a values-led culture, and our approach to inclusion and diversity means that we all have the opportunity to make a real difference, together. As part of the Group's commitments as a result of ring-fencing legislation, colleagues based in the Islands are required to be exclusively dedicated to the non-ring-fenced bank and its subsidiaries. This means that colleagues who are based in the Islands would not be able to undertake roles for the Ring Fenced Bank from their existing location and would need to consider relocation when applying for roles.

title: Data Engineer - Systematic Hedge Fund - £250k+
company: Paragon Alpha - Hedge Fund Talent Business
jd_text: Paragon Alpha is partnered with one of Europe’s leading hedge funds, a firm that has effectively integrated technology and trading to generate strong, consistent returns while building an outstanding engineering culture.The data function sits within their systematic technology division and is responsible for developing and enhancing the infrastructure that underpins their quantitative investment strategies. In this position, you will architect and scale a robust data platform, streamline ETL/ELT workflows, and enhance data processing performance across the business.This is a fantastic opportunity to collaborate directly with Quant PMs and researchers within a large, established hedge fund on the systematic side of the business.Tech: Python, SQL, AWSPlease apply if interested.
